narrative:
First Warn Forecast
Wednesday, May 20, 2026
First Warn Headlines
Cooler-than-average temperatures settle into the region through Friday.
Clouds will linger at times today and Thursday, especially south of I-70.
Multiple rounds of showers and thunderstorms are expected over the upcoming Memorial Day weekend.
Locally heavy rainfall may become a concern Friday into the weekend, especially across southeast Missouri and southwest Illinois.

A much cooler and more refreshing air mass has settled into Missouri and Illinois behind yesterday’s cold front. Early morning temperatures have dropped into the 50s across much of the region, and scattered light showers continue to develop along and north of the frontal boundary early this morning. These showers will gradually shift southward through mid to late morning before drier conditions begin to take hold later today.

A weak upper-level ridge will build into the Midwest through tonight, helping to quiet things down for the remainder of today into early Thursday. Even so, plenty of cloud cover is expected to hang around, particularly across southeast Missouri and southern Illinois where lower cloud ceilings may linger much of the day. With the clouds and cooler northerly flow in place, temperatures today will struggle to warm, with highs mainly remaining in the 60s area-wide. Some locations across central Missouri and west-central Illinois may briefly touch near 70 degrees where clouds thin out later this afternoon.

By Thursday, winds gradually shift more easterly as high pressure drifts across the Great Lakes. Temperatures will moderate slightly, with highs ranging from the middle 60s to middle 70s depending on cloud coverage and location. Overall, Thursday looks mainly dry through much of the daytime hours.

Attention then turns toward the Memorial Day weekend as a more active weather pattern develops across the Midwest. Southwest flow aloft will return Friday into the weekend, allowing several disturbances to track across Missouri and Illinois. The first notable round of showers and thunderstorms is expected late Thursday night into Friday. Atmospheric moisture levels will increase significantly, with precipitable water values approaching 2 inches at times, supporting periods of heavy rainfall.

At this time, the greatest risk for heavier rainfall appears to focus across southeast Missouri into southwest and southern Illinois where a frontal boundary is expected to stall nearby. Additional waves of showers and thunderstorms are expected Saturday through Memorial Day as multiple disturbances move through the region.

While widespread severe weather is not currently anticipated, a few stronger thunderstorms and localized flooding issues cannot be ruled out, especially where repeated rounds of rain occur over the same areas.

Temperatures over the holiday weekend will gradually warm back into the 70s, though clouds and rain chances should prevent any significant early summer heat from developing.

Stay with First Warn Weather for updates as we continue to track the evolving Memorial Day weekend forecast across the St. Louis metro, central Missouri, southeast Missouri, and southern Illinois.
